title: Selecting text
drDigitalIntro:
Selecting text tells the computer which words to work with.
• Click and drag across words to select them.
• Double-click for one word; triple-click for a whole line.
• Or press Ctrl+A to select everything at once.

playgroundTask.instructions: Two sentences sneaked in that don't belong. Select each one and press Delete to remove it — try double-clicking a word, then holding Shift and pressing the right arrow key to stretch your selection across the whole sentence.
playgroundTask.startingText: The weather is nice today. PLEASE DELETE THIS ENTIRE SENTENCE. I think I will go for a walk. THIS PART DOES NOT BELONG HERE EITHER. Maybe I will bring my dog along.
playgroundTask.correctText: The weather is nice today. I think I will go for a walk. Maybe I will bring my dog along.
mustInclude: I think I will go for a walk.
Maybe I will bring my dog along.
mustNotInclude: THIS PART DOES NOT BELONG HERE

drDigitalSuccess: You selected and deleted whole sentences — that's the power of selecting text!
drDigitalHint: The two ALL-CAPS sentences don't belong. Click at the start of one, hold Shift, and click at the end to select it all, then press Delete. Don't forget to remove the extra space left behind too.
